description:
You are a QA engineer preparing a tiny “echo” web-service test environment in your home directory.  
Set up everything exactly as described below.  The automated checker will look for the files, the precise
contents, and the alias line exactly as written—capitalisation, spacing and quoting all matter.

Goal:
1.  Directory: /home/user/qa_echo  
2.  Configuration file: /home/user/qa_echo/echo.env containing **exactly** these three lines (no extra spaces or blank lines):  
   ECHO_PORT=9090  
   ECHO_MODE=testing  
   ECHO_TIMEOUT=5  
3.  Bash alias: append the single line shown below to the very end of /home/user/.bashrc (create the file if it does not exist).  
   alias echo-test="python3 -m http.server 9090 --directory /home/user/qa_echo"  
   • The alias keyword must be lowercase.  
   • Use double quotes around the entire command, exactly as above.  
   • Do not add any extra characters, comment symbols, or whitespace at the end of the line.  
4.  Verification log: /home/user/qa_echo/setup.log written in YAML with the **exact** 4-line content below  
   (indentation is two spaces; all keys and values are lowercase; keys are followed by a single colon and a 
    single space; wrap the path value in double quotes exactly as shown).  
   setup:  
     config_file: "/home/user/qa_echo/echo.env"  
     bash_alias_added: "yes"  
     status: "ok"  

Constraints & tips:  
• Aim to accomplish everything with a simple sequence of 2-3 shell commands; however, you may use more if needed.  
• You do NOT need root or sudo.  
• The checker will confirm:  
  – directory exists,  
  – echo.env has those three lines in that order,  
  – .bashrc ends with the alias line shown (no trailing blank lines after it),  
  – setup.log content is byte-for-byte identical to the 4-line sample above (including the newline after the last line).  
• Feel free to display terminal output; the checker ignores stdout/stdin—only the file system state matters.  
• When finished, double-check every character in all created/modified files.
truth:
State required for success:

1. Directory /home/user/qa_echo must exist.

2. File /home/user/qa_echo/echo.env  
   Exact 3-line contents (**no trailing spaces, no blank line at EOF**):  
ECHO_PORT=9090
ECHO_MODE=testing
ECHO_TIMEOUT=5

3. File /home/user/.bashrc  
   The **very last** line in the file must be exactly:  
alias echo-test="python3 -m http.server 9090 --directory /home/user/qa_echo"  
   (There must be a single newline after this line; nothing follows.)

4. File /home/user/qa_echo/setup.log  
   Exact 4-line YAML content (**each line ends with a single newline; no extra blank line at end**):  
setup:
  config_file: "/home/user/qa_echo/echo.env"
  bash_alias_added: "yes"
  status: "ok"

The automated tests will:
• Stat and read /home/user/qa_echo/echo.env to match the three expected lines.  
• Read /home/user/.bashrc, split into lines, confirm the alias line is the final line and matches exactly.  
• Read /home/user/qa_echo/setup.log and compare byte-for-byte with the YAML block above.  
• Confirm no extra files or directories are required, but their presence will not cause failure as long as the items above match.

Permissions: default user-level permissions (e.g., 644 for files, 755 for directories) are sufficient; tests will not check mode bits.
